codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking gap: GET /spikes/:id now applies hasScope(auth, 'GET') after auth validation, matching other authenticated GET routes. The added tests verify write-scoped keys are denied and read/full-scoped keys are allowed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker-backend skill requires TDD red-phase and manual wrangler verification, but this fix run reported followedProcedure=true without those steps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session c2ae140e-26f9-4aa2-8a0c-2037eabb178a shows implementation edit in worker/src/index.ts before new tests were added, and verification only includes npx tsc --noEmit + npx vitest run. Handoff skillFeedback marks followedProcedure=true with no deviations.

summary: PASS: Reviewing both diffs confirms the original failure from d59427a (missing scope check on GET /spikes/:id) is resolved by fe0c33d. The route now denies write-scoped API keys with 403 SCOPE_DENIED and retains expected access for read/full scopes and admin token flows.
